member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Index
member.summary: Represent a type can be used to index a collection either from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Construct an Index using a value and indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.param: The index value. it has to be zero or positive number.
Indicating if the index is from the start or from the end.
member.remarks: If the Index constructed from the end, index value 1 means pointing at the last element and index value 0 means pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: P:System.Index.Start
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at first element.

member.name: P:System.Index.End
member.summary: Create an Index pointing at beyond last element.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromStart(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the start at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the start.

member.name: M:System.Index.FromEnd(System.Int32)
member.summary: Create an Index from the end at the position indicated by the value.
member.param: The index value from the end.

member.name: P:System.Index.Value
member.summary: Returns the index value.

member.name: P:System.Index.IsFromEnd
member.summary: Indicates whether the index is from the start or the end.

member.name: M:System.Index.GetOffset(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculate the offset from the start using the giving collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the Index will be used with. length has to be a positive value
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter and the returned offset value against negative values. we don't validate either the returned offset is greater than the input length. It is expected Index will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. If the returned offset is negative and then used to index a collection will get out of range exception which will be same affect as the validation.

The icon of the achievement shown in the popup and in the achievement list. Should be 256x128 and should respect the curved rectangular shape of the popup frame. If left null, uses a default sprite.
The LANGUAGE KEY of the description shown in the achievements list before you unlock this achievement.
The LANGUAGE KEY of the description shown in the achievements list after you unlock this achievement.
The amount of times a specific task must be completed in order for the achievement to be unlocked.
For achievements where total tasks > 1. Whether to show the completion as 'X/Y' or as 'N%".
Achievements marked as "technical" will NEVER be seen by the player and are only used for game logic.
The action that is executed when the achievement is complete.

member.name: T:ArchitectsLibrary.MonoBehaviours.AlienPowerSource
member.summary: Base class for alien power sources, which can provide energy for buildable alien modules.

member.name: P:ArchitectsLibrary.MonoBehaviours.AlienPowerSource.Power
member.summary: Total power in the system, can range from to .

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.MonoBehaviours.AlienPowerSource.maxPower
member.summary: The maximum amount of power in the system. DO NOT WRITE TO THIS FIELD. Please use instead; this field only exists for Unity serialization. If you do write, the existing amount of power in the system will not be clamped. This is impossible to do outside of the AlienPowerSource class, due to the readonly nature of the Power property. Note: This is why encapsulation is important! Please excuse me for using bad programming practices. It's not my fault, I am a slave to Unity's serialization system. Do you remember why I wrote this huge description? Because I want you to NOT WRITE TO THIS FIELD! And that means you, too, Unity!

member.name: T:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags
member.summary: Defines how a placeable can be placed.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.Ground
member.summary: Can be placed on floors. Every placeable should include at least one of the the Ground, Walls, or Ceilings flags.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.Walls
member.summary: Can be placed on walls. Every placeable should include at least one of the the Ground, Walls, or Ceilings flags.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.Ceiling
member.summary: Can be placed on ceilings. Every placeable should include at least one of the the Ground, Walls, or Ceilings flags.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.Inside
member.summary: Can be placed indoors. Every placeable should include either the Inside our Outside flag, or both.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.Outside
member.summary: Can be placed outside. Every placeable should include either the Inside our Outside flag, or both.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.AlignWithSurface
member.summary: Whether the poster aligns with surfaces. Used for wall-mounted things such as posters, mainly. If this flag is included, the forward direction (+Z) should be facing the player.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.AllowedOnRigidbody
member.summary: Allowed in Cyclops, Sea Voyager, etc... Always include this flag unless there are interesting errors that come out of placing it in a cyclops.

member.name: F:ArchitectsLibrary.Templates.PlaceableTemplate.PlacementFlags.AllowedOnConstructable
member.summary: Allowed on constructed entities such as tables, desks, shelves, etc... Include this unless you want to limit your decoration to the floor only.
